page: Amazon Web Services i7i.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing Intel Xeon 8559C processors running at 2.4 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 320 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage consists of 22,500 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and the network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 56 Gbps. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core capabilities and high storage-related performance. The instance achieves top-tier status in Passmark database operations and static web serving throughput. Multi-threaded decompression via bzip3 and Geekbench multi-core metrics also rank in the top 10% of tested systems. While single-core stress-ng performance falls in the middle 50%, multi-core stress-ng and memory bandwidth tests consistently place in the top 25%. For LLM inference, prompt processing speeds for 7B models reach top-tier levels, whereas text generation speeds for 135M and 70B models remain in the middle 50%.

The i7i.24xlarge offers high resource density by combining a substantial DDR5 memory footprint with massive local NVMe SSD storage. This hardware profile is optimized for data-intensive workloads where local storage speed and capacity are critical, offsetting the moderate single-core CPU speeds. The presence of AMX instructions provides hardware-accelerated matrix operations without requiring dedicated GPUs. This server is suited for large-scale database deployments, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and data decompression pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services i7i.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor at 2.4 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory and 22,500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, with a baseline network bandwidth of 56 Gbps. Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded decompression, alongside strong memory bandwidth. While single-core CPU performance and certain LLM text generation speeds are average, the instance excels in multi-core execution and data-intensive tasks. The inclusion of Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) supports matrix math workloads without dedicated GPUs. This configuration is suited for large databases, high-throughput web applications, and intensive data processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 22,500 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database and multi-core decompression performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 22,500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-density storage and memory configuration featuring massive local NVMe capacity and advanced matrix acceleration for data-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8559C processors.
768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 22,500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
Top-tier benchmark performance in database operations and static web serving.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated matrix and vector math.
56 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor.
Suited for large-scale databases, software compilation, and multi-threaded data pipelines.
